Member-only story

6 min read
2 hours ago

--

Difficulty: Easy
Topic: String simulation, game theory
📄 View original problem statement
Problem Summary
A colony of frogs lives on a lily pad pond. There’s one Alpha Frog (represented by ‘A’) and zero or more Beta Frogs (represented by ‘B’). The Alpha Frog starts at the leftmost lily pad and wants to reach the rightmost one.
The Alpha can move right in two ways:

• Leap over one Beta Frog if there’s an empty pad (‘.’) directly to the right of the Beta

• Move to an empty pad directly to the right
The question is: can the Alpha Frog reach the rightmost lily pad?
Key insight: This is a reachability problem where we simulate valid moves and check if we can get the Alpha from its starting position to the end.
Understanding the Problem
Let’s walk through a few examples:
Example 1: A.

• Alpha is at position 0, empty pad at position 1

• Alpha can move right to the empty pad

• Alpha reaches position 1 (the end) → YES
Example 2: AB.

• Alpha at 0, Beta at 1, empty at 2
